element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary melee weapon available from start
Ranged weapon with unlimited supply; upgradeable to larger versions via power-ups
Changes player clothes color; grants enhanced shuriken size or movement speed; reverts to base state on damage if not in red clothes
Throws bombs; more aggressive than basic soldiers
Can breathe fire; introduces elemental hazard type
Repeats 2-3 times across run; seasonal progression triggers after each rescue (summer→fall→winter→summer cycle)

element_introduction_order.first_appears: NES/Revised version final boss (second run cycle)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Unique enemy type; only non-jumping enemy; final challenge for extended play

typical_tension_triggers: Loss of power-up state (reversion to red clothes on damage)
Elevated enemy density in fortress/castle stages
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced progression through themed environments with punctuated boss encounters; tension builds across each level zone and releases at rescue checkpoints; cycles repeat with increasing difficulty

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Secret passageway as hidden stage between forest and fortress

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory combat with basic soldiers in forest setting; teaches sword and shuriken mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of specialized enemy types (red ninja bombers, fire-breathing monks); boss encounters with warlord Yoshiro; multiple rescue checkpoints maintain progression momentum
difficulty_curve.late_game: Castle assault with concentrated enemy density; encounter with evil samurai Yukinosuke; NES version extends with giant cobra final boss on second cycle
notable_spikes: Second run cycle introduction (giant green cobra)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game progresses through distinct environmental zones in linear sequence: Forest → Secret Passageway → Fortress Wall → Castle Interior, with each zone introducing new enemy types and escalating challenge
After each princess rescue, the game transitions seasons (summer → fall → winter → summer) and replays the level cycle with the same structure but higher difficulty; extends replayability
Each level zone culminates in or contains a rescue opportunity; boss encounters guard progression; clear narrative checkpoint every 1-2 levels
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 rescue, mid-game warlord encounter, late-game samurai encounter

notable_level_patterns.description: Crystal ball power-ups grant temporary stat upgrades (shuriken size, movement speed) with visual feedback (color change); damage reverts state but does not kill player in upgraded state, creating risk/reward for aggressive play
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Fortress Wall and Castle stages where power-ups are critical

sources_used: wikipedia (Legend of Kage - gameplay section, development notes, NES version specifics)
tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - regional differences, NES port confirmation)
